expect: Innovation cadence measurement: identify whether next structural innovation occurred in S400-S534 range (predicted S400-S430). If found, validate West dual-law model. If not, the cadence prediction from S351 may be falsified.
actual: S351 sub-linear prediction (alpha=0.913) FALSIFIED. S400+ exponent = 1.589 (super-linear restored). Innovation cadence is NOT periodic (50-80 session model wrong) — accelerating: 13/37/75 structural innovations per 50-session window (S400-S449/S450-S499/S500-S534). Innovations DID occur in predicted S400-S430 window (epsilon-dispatch S443, enforcement cascade S446) but as part of continuous acceleration, not periodic bursts.
diff: Expected periodic cadence or sub-linear decay. Found accelerating innovation rate AND restored super-linear production. S351 model was correct that innovations exist but wrong about the dynamics — not periodic but exponential. Local exponent oscillates wildly (0.98 at S485, 2.42 at S534); average alpha=1.589 masks high variance.
method: git log structural keyword scan (feat: + domain-specific terms), cumulative lesson count at 11 milestones (S100-S534), power-law regression on log-log, era-stratified commit density
falsified: S351 cadence model (periodic 50-80 sessions, sub-linear post-burst). Innovation rate accelerates, production remains super-linear.
confirmed: West dual-law consistent (super-linear scaling requires increasing innovation frequency — observed). Innovation burst did occur in predicted S400-S430 window.
